lesson: Phase 3 startup requires resolving INT-001 first: packages/desktop typecheck fails because ipc.ts imports ./github-ipc and ./ipc-validation which don't exist on disk. These files were promised in the Phase 2 handoff but never committed.
context: Roadmap: INT-001, INT-002, INT-003 all active. Desktop typecheck fails. IPC tests fail due to missing modules.

lesson: 10 OpenCode GitHub tests fail due to extractResponseText returning null instead of text value, and formatPromptTooLargeError formatting defects. These are production bugs unrelated to Phase 2 frozen lanes.
context: packages/opencode/test/cli/github-action.test.ts: 10 failures in extractResponseText and formatPromptTooLargeError

lesson: The coordination bus events (TaskStatusChanged, PathClaimed, SessionHeartbeat, ActivityLogged) are defined in coord-events.ts but NEVER published. Any TEL work must plumb Bus.publish() calls alongside existing DuckDB persistence.
context: TEL-001 cartography — discovered while mapping task.ts + coordination.ts surface. coord-events.ts exists, Bus.Service is wired in ToolRegistry, but nothing calls Bus.publish(TaskStatusChanged, ...).

lesson: TaskStatusChanged BusEvent was already defined in coord-events.ts but never published. Always check if the event definition exists before creating a new one — the schema, registration, and layer provisioning may already be done.
context: TEL-001: coord-events.ts had TaskStatusChanged defined but task.ts never called bus.publish

lesson: When adding cache to Effect tool execute closures, both the Interface.getOrCompute and the execute assignment need explicit generic R type parameters and `as any` casts because Effect v4's invariant R type doesn't flow through intermediate cache services.
context: Phase 1 ToolCache, tool.ts wrap() function, cache.ts Interface

lesson: Background tasks via `task(background: true)` can complete but not appear on the task_board dashboard. The handoff messages may not be sent via send_message — check both the task result and the filesystem for actual changes made.
context: Executor launched with background:true for Phase 1 ToolCache — completed but not visible on task_board until checking specific session_id

lesson: When refactoring desktop/src/main/ipc.ts, extracted modules (github-ipc.ts, ipc-validation.ts) must be created in the same commit to avoid broken imports and typecheck failures.
context: INT-001: Phase 2 refactoring extracted OAuth and validation code into separate files but never committed the new files

lesson: Contention detection for produce_fragment uses 3-tier anchor overlap heuristic: numeric lines >50 apart are compatible, named regions classify by purpose (imports/exports/return/switch), mixed numeric+named is conservatively flagged as warning
context: CI-002 contention module in packages/opencode/src/coordination/contention.ts

lesson: Background lane-owners writing to the same file (coord-events.ts) cause data loss when one overwrites the other — always launch a dedicated consolidator agent when lane-owners share a file, or use produce_fragment protocol.
context: Session 2: CI-002 wrote coord-events.ts with 5 events; TEL-002 overwrote with only SubagentPhaseChanged

lesson: TEL-002 phase events must be wired into the tool execution path in session/tools.ts (where tools are dispatched), not in a standalone file. The TEL-002 lane-owner correctly created publish helpers in execution.ts AND integrated them into tools.ts.
context: Session 2: TEL-002 integrated into tools.ts resolve() function with publishStarted/publishCompleted/publishFailed around tool execution

lesson: In Effect v4, use Effect.catch(errorHandler) not Effect.catchAll(errorHandler). The catch handler returns Effect<T, never, R> not T directly. Extra closing parens in deeply nested Effect.gen blocks cause TS1109/TS1128 syntax errors that are hard to spot.
context: Session 2: tools.ts had extra `),` at end of Effect.catch block causing 4 syntax errors

lesson: OpenCode Desktop is ~65% decoupled: centralized IPC handlers, typed preload bridge, strong theme system, but preload is a 50-method monolith, tests are thin (1 file), and platform path handling mixes app.getPath with manual os.homedir() calls.
context: Audit session: read 19+ source files across packages/desktop, app, ui

lesson: The theme system in packages/ui/src/theme/ is genuinely world-class — 38 themes, Oklch color science, two engine modes (seeds & palette), 200+ design tokens, CSS variable output, SolidJS context provider. This matches Linear/VSCode quality.
context: Inspected resolve.ts (541 lines), types.ts (71 lines), all 38 theme JSON files

lesson: OpenCode Desktop's main UX gaps vs industry leaders (Raycast/Linear) are: no command palette, no keyboard shortcut discoverability, no systematic animation design system, skeleton loading states, empty states, and no onboarding flow. Architecture is solid — UX surface needs the most investment.
context: Cross-reference with VSCode, Raycast, Linear, Arc UX patterns

lesson: Effect v4 uses Effect.catch(error, handler) not Effect.catchAll. The catch handler receives (error: unknown) and must return the same Effect type as the original.
context: TEL-002 session/tools.ts — Effect.catchAll doesn't exist in effect@4.0.0-beta.66

lesson: Effect v4 Semaphore API: Semaphore.make(n) returns an Effect, semaphore.withPermit(effect) uses 1 permit, semaphore.withPermits(n)(effect) uses N permits. No .available() method exists.
context: Phase 2 semaphore wrapping in session/tools.ts. User instruction had incorrect API; verified against actual effect/dist/Semaphore.d.ts types.

lesson: OpenCode i18n uses flat dot-notation dictionaries with @solid-primitives/i18n v2.2.1. en.ts (963 keys) is merged with @opencode-ai/ui/i18n/en.ts (152 keys). All 17 non-English locales are missing 72-113 keys. The translator function t('key', params) is created once in LanguageProvider context via i18n.translator(() => dict, i18n.resolveTemplate).
context: i18n cartography session — discovered the full structure, library API, and locale parity gaps

lesson: Never use button label string comparison for Electron dialog routing when localizing buttons — use the button index from result.response instead, which is invariant across locales.
context: plan: i18n-parity-platform-polish, file: windows.ts:276

lesson: Only extract constants that are truly duplicated (used 2+ times in non-i18n, non-test source files) — avoid premature abstraction of single-use values into a shared hub
context: Plan review caught that 27 of 29 proposed constants were single-use. Scoped to only duplicated values.

lesson: When adding ErrorBoundary fallbacks, reuse existing ErrorPage component instead of creating a parallel ErrorFallback to avoid two divergent maintenance paths
context: Critic caught that ErrorFallback would duplicate the 371-line ErrorPage. Used ErrorPage directly.

lesson: In SolidJS apps, self-contained inline sub-components at the bottom of component files (composer picker, etc.) are the easiest extraction targets — they have no closure variable dependencies and can be extracted with zero behavior change.
context: Extracted 5 composer picker components (~222 lines) from prompt-input.tsx (2,172 lines). New file passed typecheck with zero errors.

lesson: The electron npm package is a native C++ module that cannot be imported in bun's test environment. Use inline logic replication for testing Electron-dependent code in bun:test.
context: Lane C — desktop test infrastructure

lesson: For Electron apps tested with bun:test, use self-contained logic tests that replicate pure function patterns inline, since mock.module does not intercept the electron native module.
context: Lane C — discovered during desktop test setup

lesson: Animation tokens should be added as CSS variables in v2/styles/theme.css (not in ResolvedTheme type) since they are static across all themes — no type widening or breaking changes needed.
context: Lane D — Design system completion: animation tokens and missing components

lesson: Kobalte 0.13.11 Slider.ValueLabel does not accept a render-function child pattern; use a native <output> element and track value via onChange signal instead.
context: Lane D — slider-v2.tsx ValueLabel type error

lesson: Locale files in this repo have multi-line key values. Simple regex-based key extraction corrupts them; always use targeted append-before-close instead of full file reconstruction.
context: Lane E: fill_i18n_gaps.py corrupted es.ts/ja.ts due to multi-line values. Fixed with targeted insertion.

lesson: Linux custom titlebar requires extending both updateTitlebar's platform guard AND createMainWindow/createLoadingWindow frame options. titleBarOverlay may throw on Wayland - wrap in try-catch.
context: Lane E: Added Linux to windows.ts titlebar path

lesson: When localizing Electron dialog button labels, use result.response index for routing, not string comparison against button labels.
context: Lane E: handle() function in windows.ts used button === "Export Logs" comparison

lesson: When using `as const` for string constants as default function parameters, TypeScript infers the narrow literal type, not the union — use explicit `: string` annotations to accept all values.
context: getStore(name = IPC.store.SETTINGS) inferred name type as literal "opencode.settings" instead of string, causing TS errors when passing other store names.

lesson: Restoring deleted files with `git restore` recovers HEAD version only — any uncommitted dirty changes are lost. Always verify restored file has expected content.
context: preload/types.ts was dirty-deleted; restore from HEAD lost deleteCustomAgent and McpServerEntry type additions that had to be re-applied.

lesson: A pure IPC channels registry file (zero imports, const string constants) is the cleanest way to eliminate magic strings in Electron apps — safe for preload sandbox, enables TypeScript narrowing via keyof typeof, and a single grep confirms full coverage.
context: Lane A created ipc-channels.ts: 57 handle + 4 send + 6 push + 5 store channels, zero imports, zero magic strings remaining in desktop/src/

lesson: To run 6+ lane-owners in parallel with zero file conflicts, scope each lane to a distinct directory tree (e.g. desktop/src/main/, app/src/, ui/src/, app/src/components/, app/src/i18n/). The top-level directory is the conflict boundary.
context: 6 parallel lanes, 60+ files modified across 3 packages, zero merge conflicts

lesson: The electron npm package is a native C++ module — bun test cannot import it (even mock.module doesn't intercept .node binaries). Test strategy: inline pure function logic replication for isolated unit tests, not Electron API mocking.
context: Lane C discovered that all existing electron-dependent tests fail in bun test environment. Worked around by testing pure logic functions.

lesson: When lanes create files that other lanes need to test, schedule test writing AFTER all upstream lanes complete. Lane C wrote IPC tests but github-ipc.ts didn't exist yet — had to relaunch post-hoc.
context: Lane C deferred github-ipc and ipc-validation tests. Lane A created them. Relaunched test task post-hoc.

lesson: When designing future UX, ground every component in existing codebase architecture — reference actual providers, import paths, and component patterns. Future sessions should read the vision document from docs/json/opencode/ux-vision/ to understand the north star before implementing new features.
context: Designed 3 visions for OpenCode Desktop. Discovered rich existing infrastructure: session-viz, CommandProvider, NotificationProvider, persisted() store pattern, and 60+ UI components. Visions build on these rather than starting from scratch.

lesson: Session UI components use data-component attributes for CSS targeting (not CSS modules). Animation tokens from theme.css are available as --v2-duration-* and --v2-ease-*.
context: Created timeline-scrubber.tsx and timeline-scrubber.css matching @opencode-ai/ui/v2 design system patterns

lesson: Creating native Effect-based Tool.Def implementations requires: (1) Schema.Struct for parameters, (2) Tool.define with Effect.gen for service acquisition, (3) execute returning Effect with .pipe(Effect.orDie), (4) registration in registry.ts imports + yield* + Tool.init + builtin array
context: Lane 0e: 4 gap tools created as native Tool.Def

lesson: Agent lifecycle phases described in .md prompts are suggestions, not enforcement — all agents use the same generic LLM streaming loop regardless of their described wave sequence.
context: Analyzed 10+ agent .md profiles (cartographer, architect, critic, executor, validator, stress, lane-owner, orchestrator, repairer) and confirmed all use identical processor loop at packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts

lesson: Agent type is stored as a plain string with no discriminated union — there is no code-level distinction between a cartographer, critic, or executor session; only prompt + permissions differ.
context: Agent.Info in packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts has no 'type' or 'category' field — all agents are structurally identical at the code level

lesson: The entire wave-based agent lifecycle (cartographer → architect → critic → gate → executor → validator → stress) is a prompt-only convention with zero runtime enforcement — sub-subagents referenced in .md profiles may not actually exist as runnable agent types.
context: Comprehensive analysis of 10+ agent .md profiles + agent.ts + processor.ts confirmed identical generic loop for all agent types

lesson: The 8 adversary profiles share ~95% identical structure (persona, output format, article sections) — could be consolidated into a parameterized adversary generator with domain parameter.
context: Read claim-adversary, evidence-adversary, authority-adversary, security-adversary .md files — all follow identical template

lesson: Every agent type defines a different handoff format in prose (executor: JSON with verification, critic: Markdown tables, architect: JSON fixes/risks). verify_handoff validates a generic contract but can't validate per-type completeness.
context: Compared executor handoff format, critic verdict format, architect plan format, lane-owner handoff format across .md profiles

lesson: The session processor has a two-layer architecture: OUTER loop (prompt.ts) handles agent lifecycle (switching, compaction, subtask routing), INNER loop (processor.ts) is a pure LLM streaming engine. Per-agent lifecycle state machines belong in the outer loop, not the processor — the inner loop should remain agent-agnostic.
context: Cartographer-B analysis of prompt.ts:1244 runLoop and processor.ts:756 process() confirmed the two-layer split. The outer loop loads session, picks agent, builds prompt, creates processor handle, processes result, and loops. Processor never touches agent config except for doom loop detection.

lesson: Watcher modules should gracefully return null when no backend is available rather than throwing; all statuses render through a single context provider that handles dismissed state and polling intervals
context: Lane 3 — Proactive Ambient Agent: all 5 watchers use graceful fallback pattern

lesson: createSimpleContext from @opencode-ai/ui/context is the standard pattern; the provider returns an object with getter properties and methods, wrapping init inside the factory function
context: 22 context files in packages/app/src/context/ follow this pattern

lesson: Task lifecycle is tracked by two independent systems: Coordination (DB-backed claim/release/fail) and BackgroundJob (in-memory SynchronizedRef with Fiber status). They track the same task but have independent state machines — DB says 'released' while BackgroundJob says 'running'. No unified task lifecycle manager.
context: Cartographer-C analysis of task.ts (Coordination.claimTask/releaseTask/failTask) and background/job.ts (background.start with independently tracked status). The two systems overlap in purpose but share no state machine.

lesson: Background task results are injected into the parent session via ops.prompt() wrapped in Effect.ignore. If the injection fails (e.g., parent session closed), the completion is silently lost. No retry, no escalation, no alert.
context: task.ts: background lifecycle wraps the completion injection in Effect.forkIn(scope, {startImmediately: true}).Effect.ignore — fire-and-forget with no result tracking

lesson: TimelineScrubber uses TurnSummary + turns prop; ensure imports use exact type names from the scrubber component
context: Lane 1 — agent-intelligence-dag: Had to align TurnSummary vs ScrubberSegment types between message-timeline.tsx and timeline-scrubber.tsx

lesson: Agent heartbeat tracking in session-viz context needs closure-scoped agentColorMap inside init, not at module level, to avoid TS2304 errors
context: Lane 1 — agent-intelligence-dag: getAgentColor function referencing agentColorMap had to be inside init closure

lesson: When both tool.ts wrap() and session/tools.ts bridge apply semaphore/timeout/retry layers, the bridge should only apply the read/write pool semaphore and not duplicate per-tool limits already handled by wrap().
context: Lane 0d — bridge-tools.ts changes needed simplification after discovering Lane 0a's wrap() already handles per-tool config

lesson: In Effect v4 beta, the duration input type is `Duration.Input` (not `Duration.DurationInput`). Always check the .d.ts file's actual exports when using Effect v4 beta types.
context: packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts — Duration.Input type resolution

lesson: Tracking state (recents/favorites) should be embedded directly in the command context to avoid requiring a separate provider. Access via command.trackUse, command.recents, command.favorites.
context: CommandTracking was initially a separate context but required a separate provider mount. Refactored into the existing CommandContext for cleaner integration.

lesson: ToolCache upgrade: Map insertion-order LRU works via delete+re-set, Duration.toMillis() converts Duration to ms for expiry math, Clock.currentTimeMillis replaces Date.now() for testable time
context: packages/opencode/src/tool/cache.ts — ToolCache lane 0b

lesson: Importing from tool.ts in cache.ts creates cross-lane fragility — better to self-contain types when possible since another lane may delete/modify tool.ts
context: packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts was deleted by another lane during cache.ts upgrade

lesson: When using Deferred for inflight dedup in a cache, always ensure the Deferred is completed on compute failure (via Deferred.failCause or Deferred.die in Effect.onExit), otherwise all concurrent waiters hang forever.
context: cache.ts lines 111-120: Deferred created but only succeeded on success path; compute failure leaves Deferred unresolved → waiters hang permanently

lesson: Inflight dedup must atomically check-and-insert into the inflight map. Ref.get + Map.set outside Ref.update has a race window where two fibers both see no entry and both compute. Use Ref.modify or Effect.cached instead.
context: cache.ts lines 100-112: TOCTOU race between checking inflight map and inserting new Deferred

lesson: Files can be silently deleted by concurrent sessions sharing the same working tree — always check git status before and after write operations, and be prepared to restore from git HEAD if a target file disappears.
context: During Lane 0c, registry.ts and tool.ts were deleted from working tree between read and write operations. Had to restore from git HEAD and re-apply changes.

lesson: Parallel lanes that delete shared files (session/tools.ts, tool.ts, registry.ts) cause destructive cross-lane conflicts: staged git rm of a file removes all uncommitted Phase 0 changes from other lanes.
context: Lane 0f verification: session/tools.ts with semaphore split was deleted by another lane, losing all Phase 0d changes

lesson: Manual inflight dedup with Ref<Map<key, Deferred>> is error-prone. The check-then-set is not atomic, and Deferred.succeed on success-only path orphans waiters on compute failure. Use Effect.cached instead which handles scope-safe dedup with proper cleanup on all exit paths.
context: packages/opencode/src/tool/cache.ts CACHE-001, CACHE-002 found during adversarial stress testing

lesson: Never catch-all in size estimation functions. JSON.stringify throws on circular refs/BigInt — returning 0 silently lets huge objects bypass maxEntrySize filters and pollute the cache with invisible entries.
context: packages/opencode/src/tool/cache.ts line 42-48, verified via bun test

lesson: The PG migration schema definitions (.pg.sql.ts files) are 100% complete for all 7 table types. The 3% roadmap completion reflects the application layer migration (import switching + dual-write plumbing), not schema work.
context: Lane-2 inventory verified all .sql.ts ↔ .pg.sql.ts pairs exist and are type-correct. The schemas are ready to go.

lesson: Never use Ref.set(cache, new Map()) for cache invalidation when other fibers may concurrently be inside Ref.update(cache, fn) — the blind assignment in Ref.set can be undone by a stale Ref.update that read the old Map. Use Ref.update with m.clear() + return m instead to mutate in place. Same race applies to any Ref<Map> where some paths use Ref.set and others use Ref.update.
context: packages/opencode/src/tool/cache.ts concurrency-stresser finding CACHE-RACE-008: invalidate() does Ref.set(cache, new Map()) while insertion path does Ref.update(cache, fn). The blind read in Ref.update can observe the old Map before Ref.set replaces it, then overwrite the new empty Map with stale entries.

lesson: Mixing delta-based estimatedBytes updates (line 90) with full recalculations (line 156) creates inconsistency windows: if compute fails between the delta and the recalc, or if two fibers concurrently hit the same expired entry, estimatedBytes drifts from true state. Use only full recalculations, or only deltas — never both.
context: packages/opencode/src/tool/cache.ts assumption-challenger ASSUMPTION-6: estimatedBytes double-decrement race and compute-failure-after-decrement drift

lesson: In this Effect version, Schema.Literal takes exactly 1 argument (use Schema.Literals([array]) for union literals). Schema.Union takes an array, not variadic args. Use Schema.Literals for string literal unions.
context: coordination.ts rewrite: Schema.Literal("claims", "reservations", ...) failed with TS2554

lesson: Effect Schema's Literal (singular) uses rest params but may not infer correctly in TS; use Schema.Literals(array) with an array argument instead, and wrap array fields in Schema.mutable() to avoid readonly conflicts with downstream consumers.
context: packages/opencode/src/lifecycle/engine.ts, config/agent.ts, agent/agent.ts

lesson: In Effect 4, Schema.Literal takes a single value; use Schema.Literals([...]) for union literals. Use Schema.mutable(Schema.Array(...)) to avoid readonly type mismatches.
context: lane-3: lifecycle package — Schema.Literal("a","b","c") silently resolved to type "a" only, causing escalation and condition literal errors in builtins.ts

lesson: Concurrent sessions can git rm shared files, destroying uncommitted changes from other lanes. Always verify file existence + dirtiness before making changes, and use checkpoint tool to commit proven work before another lane can delete it.
context: Phase 0 — a concurrent session git rmed tool.ts, registry.ts, session/tools.ts. Lane 0c's git restore lost Lane 0a's 159 additions and Lane 0d's semaphore split. 2 repair rounds needed.

lesson: Schema.Schema.Type produces readonly types incompatible with mutable object literals — use standalone interfaces alongside schemas for compile-time constant values
context: Lane 3 lifecycle engine: definition.ts needed both Schema definitions (runtime validation) and standalone interfaces (compile-time type compatibility)

lesson: Use Schema.Literals([array]) not Schema.Literal(...args) in this project's Effect version
context: Lane 3 lifecycle engine: Schema.Literal("a","b") fails, Schema.Literals(["a","b"]) works

lesson: Use Schema.mutable(Schema.Array(...)) when defining array fields in struct schemas to avoid readonly type conflicts in downstream consumers
context: Lane 3 lifecycle engine: lifecycle field in AgentSchema needed Schema.mutable() for phases/transitions arrays

lesson: LifecycleEngine uses hook-based approach (onProcessorRun/onRepair/onPhaseEnter/onPhaseExit) instead of opaque processorHandle — this lets callers control tool gating, subagent spawning, and processor invocation without coupling the engine to those details.
context: Lane 3 — Lifecycle Engine. Rewrote engine.ts to use typed hooks.

lesson: The codebase's Effect version uses catchCause (not catchAll) with `.pipe()` for error recovery. Direct `Effect.catchAll(...)` is not available — always use `.pipe(Effect.catchCause(...))`.
context: Lane 3 — compiler errors showed catchAll doesn't exist on the Effect type in this version.

lesson: When inlining TuiEvent definitions that use Schema.Struct, remember to import Schema from "effect" and cast sessionID to SessionID branded type before calling session.get()
context: Lane R4: Fixed 12 errors in tui.ts — TuiEvent was inlined from deleted @/cli/cmd/tui/event without Schema import

lesson: When inline-stubbing context providers in test fixtures, ensure stubs for useKV, useProject, and useSync match all properties the tests access (e.g. set() signature, .workspace.set(), .data.vcs, .session.refresh/sync)
context: Lane R7: sync test files had type errors because inline stubs in sync-fixture.tsx were too minimal for the consuming tests

lesson: Calling spyOn creates a Mock object that replaces the original function. Do NOT add () after mockResolvedValue() — that calls the mock and stores a Promise instead of the Mock, causing mockRestore to fail with TS2339.
context: R2 plugin-loader-entrypoint.test.ts: 18 errors from adding () after mockResolvedValue() calls

lesson: When replacing deleted source imports with local type stubs, use bracket index access (T["key"]) instead of dot access (T.key) for type members, because the type and const share a name — the type cannot be used as a namespace.
context: Repair lane R5: TS2713 errors in test/fixture/tui-runtime.ts where TuiConfig.Resolved (type) was used with dot access

lesson: TypeScript wildcard module declarations (`declare module "*.sql"`) only provide default exports. Named imports from `*.pg.sql` paths fail because they match the wildcard rather than resolving to `*.sql.ts` files. Use `.sql` (not `.pg.sql`) for imports that need to resolve to `.sql.ts` source files.
context: packages/opencode/src/storage/schema.ts — 11 TS2614 errors fixed by restoring import paths from .pg.sql to .sql
